items.title: Elon Musk unveils chip manufacturing plans for SpaceX and Tesla
items.summary: Elon Musk recently outlined ambitious plans for a chip-building collaboration Tesla and SpaceX — but he has a history of overpromising.

items.title: TechCrunch Mobility: Uber everywhere, all at once
items.summary: Welcome back to TechCrunch Mobility, your central hub for news and insights on the future of transportation.

items.title: Delve accused of misleading customers with ‘fake compliance’
items.summary: An anonymous Substack post accuses compliance startup Delve of “falsely” convincing “hundreds of customers they were compliant” with privacy and security regulations.

items.title: An exclusive tour of Amazon’s Trainium lab, the chip that’s won over Anthropic, OpenAI, even Apple
items.summary: Shortly after Amazon  announced its $50 billion investment in OpenAI, AWS invited me on a private tour of the chip lab at the heart of the deal.

items.title: Are AI tokens the new signing bonus or just a cost of doing business?
items.summary: Maybe tokens really will become the fourth pillar of engineering compensation. But engineers might want to hold the line before embracing this as a straightforward win.

items.title: It’s been 20 years since the first tweet
items.summary: On March 21, 2006, Jack Dorsey posted a simple message: “just setting up my twittr”.

items.title: Publisher pulls horror novel ‘Shy Girl’ over AI concerns
items.summary: Hachette Book Group said it will not be publishing “Shy Girl” over concerns that artificial intelligence was used to generate the text.

items.title: Why Wall Street wasn’t won over by Nvidia’s big conference
items.summary: Despite investor fears of an AI bubble, Nvidia's latest conference shows that most in the industry aren't concerned by that possibility.

items.title: How fusion power works and the startups pursuing it
items.summary: Fusion power promises to generate large amounts of clean electricity from nearly limitless fuel. This article explains the main approaches and the companies that use them.

items.title: New court filing reveals Pentagon told Anthropic the two sides were nearly aligned — a week after Trump declared the relationship kaput
items.summary: Anthropic submitted two sworn declarations to a California federal court late Friday afternoon, pushing back on the Pentagon's assertion that the AI company poses an "unacceptable risk to national security" and arguing that the government's case relies on technical misunderstandings and claims that were never actually raised during the months of negotiations.

items.title: Elon Musk misled Twitter investors while trying to get out of acquisition, jury says
items.summary: At the time, Musk had tweeted that Twitter had too many bots, which is why he was trying to renege on his commitment to buy the company.

items.title: Microsoft rolls back some of its Copilot AI bloat on Windows
items.summary: The company is reducing Copilot entry points on Windows, starting with Photos, Widgets, Notepad, and other apps.

items.title: What happened at Nvidia GTC: NemoClaw, Robot Olaf, and a $1 trillion bet
items.summary: CEO Jensen Huang took the stage at Nvidia’s GTC conference this week in his signature leather jacket to deliver a two-and-a-half-hour keynote, projecting $1 trillion in AI chip sales through 2027, declaring that every company needs an “OpenClaw strategy,” and closing with a rambling Olaf robot that had to get its mic cut. The message was hard to miss: Nvidia […]

items.title: Amid legal turmoil, Kalshi is temporarily banned in Nevada
items.summary: It's the latest escalation in a regulatory battle over the future of prediction markets.

items.title: Pinterest CEO calls on governments to ban social media for users under 16
items.summary: Pinterest’s chief compares social media to tobacco and alcohol, saying children need similar protections.

items.title: Why scientists can’t get a laugh
items.summary: Humor is hard to pull off in front of a cold audience, full stop.

items.title: A French Navy officer accidentally leaked the location of an aircraft carrier by logging his run on Strava
items.summary: A French naval officer went on a run around the deck of the Charles de Gaulle aircraft carrier, inadvertently leaking the warship's location when he uploaded the workout to Strava.

items.title: WordPress.com now lets AI agents write and publish posts, and more
items.summary: New AI agents on WordPress.com could lower barriers to publishing while increasing machine-generated content across the web.

items.title: Jeff Bezos’ Blue Origin enters the space data center game
items.summary: "Project Sunrise" would include more than 50,000 satellites performing high-energy compute on orbit.

items.title: US accuses Iran’s government of operating hacktivist group that hacked Stryker
items.summary: The U.S. Justice Department said an Iranian security ministry operates the fake activist persona known as Handala, which claimed responsibility for the destructive hack targeting medical tech giant Stryker.
